channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump posts AI-generated image fueling UFO speculation amid upcoming file releases
item.description: On May 18, 2026, former President Donald Trump shared an AI-generated image showing himself walking alongside a chained, grey alien figure. The post came without explanation and has prompted widesprea...

item.title: Winnipeg Liquor Mart Resumes Sales of U.S. Liquors Amid Limited Supply
item.description: The Winnipeg Liquor Mart has resumed selling U.S.-produced alcoholic beverages, though only while supplies last. This follows a period where these products were unavailable, likely due to supply chain...

item.title: Adan Abdulla Mohamed Appointed as KRA Commissioner General for Three-Year Term
item.description: Treasury CS John Mbadi has officially appointed Adan Abdulla Mohamed as the new Commissioner General of the Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA), taking over from Humphrey Wattanga. The appointment, which is...

item.title: Rebecca Leslie’s late goal keeps Ottawa Charge alive in Walter Cup final
item.description: In a thrilling Game 3 of the Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) Walter Cup final, the Ottawa Charge narrowly avoided elimination with a dramatic 2-1 comeback victory over the Montreal Victoire....

item.title: Xenophobia in South Africa: Immigrants Not to Blame for Socioeconomic Challenges
item.description: Over the past month, anti-immigrant protests organised by the group March and March have taken place in Durban, Pretoria, and Johannesburg, often involving violence and intimidation against people per...

item.title: Countrywide Transport Strike Continues Over High Fuel Prices
item.description: The nationwide transport strike in Kenya, led by users of petroleum products including the Matatu Owners Association, has entered its second day. The protest started on Monday, May 18, as drivers and ...

item.title: House Panel Approves Hazard Pay and Support for Civilian Personnel in West Philippine Sea
item.description: The House Special Committee on the West Philippine Sea has approved House Bill No. 8673, which provides hazard pay, insurance, medical and psychological services, and maritime safety training for civi...

item.title: Australia to invest $11 billion to extend service life of Collins-class submarines
item.description: The Australian government has committed $11 billion to extend the operational life of its six Collins-class submarines, bridging a capability gap until the AUKUS submarines are delivered. Defence Mini...

item.title: Oducado resigns from Congress citing health issues, denies impeachment pressure
item.description: Former 1Tahanan party-list representative Nat Oducado officially announced his resignation from Congress on May 19, 2026, attributing his departure to personal health concerns rather than political pr...

item.title: A Statistical Examination of Similes in Popular Fiction
item.description: Russell Samora, with design and illustration by Shelly Tan, conducted an in-depth study of similes used across popular fiction for The Pudding. Moving beyond simple word counts, the analysis explores ...

item.title: Trump Visits Beijing with Top US Business Leaders for Strategic Talks with Xi Jinping
item.description: On May 13, 2026, US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for high-level discussions with Chinese President Xi Jinping. The visit comes amid mounting global tensions related to trade, technology, ...

item.title: CHADEMA Resumes Nationwide Rallies Amid Chairperson’s Health Concerns in Prison
item.description: Tanzania’s main opposition party, CHADEMA, has restarted its public rallies across the country, demanding the release of their chairperson and calling for a new constitution as well as accountability ...

item.title: Supreme Court directs states to enforce stray dog control measures in public areas
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has declined to withdraw its previous order mandating that stray dogs picked up from public places must not be returned to the same locations after vaccination or sterilisat...

item.title: Hawthorn to end home games in Launceston as Tasmania Devils prepare AFL debut
item.description: The AFL has confirmed that Hawthorn will no longer play home matches in Tasmania after the 2027 season, clearing the way for the Tasmania Devils to enter the competition in 2028. Hawthorn have had a s...

item.title: Philippine President Marcos to Prioritize Security and Energy Ties in Japan State Visit
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is set to make a rare state visit to Japan from May 26 to May 29, 2026, emphasizing two main priorities: enhancing security cooperation and ensuring energy re...

item.title: WHO and Africa CDC raise alarm over fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has expressed serious concern over the rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak in central Africa, now declared a continental emergency. In the Democratic Republic of Congo...

item.title: Peter Obi, Tinubu, and Makinde Respond to Oyo School Abduction and Teacher’s Death
item.description: Last Friday, bandits invade Esiele community for Oriire Local Government Area, Oyo State, and abduct staff, students, and pupils from Community Grammar School, Baptist Nursery and Primary School, and ...

item.title: Government Forms Special Commission to Investigate October 2025 Election Violence
item.description: Dar es Salaam – President Samia Suluhu Hassan imeunda tume maalumu ya kisheria kuchunguza na kuwachukulia hatua wale wote waliohusika na vurugu zilizotokea baada ya uchaguzi mkuu wa Oktoba 29, 2025. T...

item.title: Practical Tips for Keeping Your Home Clean with Pets
item.description: With shedding season in full swing during mid-to-late May, pet owners often find themselves overwhelmed by hair, dander, and messes around the home. Brushing your pets regularly, especially with a too...

item.title: Northwest Territories Responds to Three Early Wildfires
item.description: The Northwest Territories government is currently managing three wildfires as the wildfire season begins, two of which were caused by human activity. A small human-caused fire in the South Slave regio...
